mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is directional only when policy is actively easing, and it fails if rate cuts coincide with recessionary deterioration in labor and activity data.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX forward returns are negative in a majority of months with falling FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03 while FEDFUNDS is declining.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, the equity market discounts tighter liquidity and a higher real discount rate, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be strongest when inflation is not falling simultaneously.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or rising.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates slowing hiring and weaker aggregate demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is not expected to work when unemployment is falling or flat.
failure_condition: Reject if 60d forward SPX returns are positive in a majority of episodes after a 0.3pp 3-month UNRATE increase, or if the effect is not stronger than unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Falling PAYEMS signals payroll contraction and weakening household income growth, which should lower near-term equity returns through weaker consumption expectations and earnings revisions. This is a short-horizon effect that should vanish when employment growth is still positive.
failure_condition: Reject if 5d SPX returns are not negative on average after monthly PAYEMS declines, or if the hit rate is not better than 50% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly retail sales growth in RSAFS signals resilient consumer demand and supports earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should be strongest when sales growth is broad-based rather than a one-off rebound.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with RSAFS growth above 0.5% m/m do not produce positive 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if the rolling 3-year IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with stable inflation indicates improving real output without overheating, which supports margins and cyclical equities over the next quarter. The interaction matters because industrial production gains are bullish only when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO changes fail to improve 60d SPX returns when CPIAUCSL is stable, or if the combined signal is not stronger than INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in HOUST after a prior slowdown signals a rebound in housing demand and construction activity, which improves cyclical sentiment and supports S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal should fail when housing starts rise only because of volatile monthly noise without follow-through.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns are not positive on average after HOUST rises above 5%, or if the effect reverses in follow-up months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth identifies a broad demand slowdown that should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The interaction is essential because a decline in only one series is not enough to confirm economy-wide weakening.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth negative and RSAFS growth negative
failure_condition: Reject if joint negative GDPC1 and RSAFS growth does not precede negative 60d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the interaction adds no predictive power beyond either series alone.
